title: 10 best herbal essential oils for menopause
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for menopause are plant-based extracts used to alleviate symptoms associated with this natural life stage. They help by reducing hot flashes, mood swings, and sleep disturbances, promoting overall well-being. The best herbal essential oils for menopause include Cimicifuga racemosa, Vitex agnus-castus, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Angelica archangelica, Valeriana officinalis, Zingiber officinale, Trifolium pratense, Avena sativa, Hypericum perforatum, and Melissa officinalis. If left untreated, menopause symptoms can negatively impact daily life, causing stress, anxiety, and decreased quality of life, ultimately affecting relationships, work, and overall health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian essential oils helps with menopause because it provides a natural solution to alleviate hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings. The oil's sedative properties can also help regulate sleep patterns, which are often disrupted during this phase of life. Additionally, valerian essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that may reduce joint pain and stiffness commonly experienced during menopause. By using valerian essential oils in aromatherapy, women can promote relaxation, reduce stress, and find relief from menopausal symptoms.
remedy_properties: Sedative property: Valerian essential oil has sedative properties that can help alleviate hot flashes, night sweats, and insomnia associated with menopause, promoting a restful sleep.

Antioxidant property: Valerian essential oil is rich in antioxidants that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to symptoms such as mood swings, anxiety, and depression during menopause.

Muscle relaxant property: Valerian essential oil has muscle-relaxing properties that can help alleviate joint pain, cramps, and spasms commonly experienced during menopause, promoting overall relaxation and comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, making them ideal for alleviating hot flashes and night sweats associated with menopause.
Rhizomes: Contain a high concentration of valerenic acid, which has a sedative effect and helps to regulate hormonal imbalance, reducing symptoms of mood swings and insomnia during menopause.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality valeriana officinalis roots at dawn on a dry day to ensure optimal potency.
Chop 500 grams of harvested roots into small pieces and dry them in a warm oven at 60°c for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried root pieces using 1000 milliliters of water and a clevenger apparatus for 4 hours and 30 minutes.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a paper coffee filter into dark glass bottles, discarding any impurities.
Store the final valeriana officinalis essential oil in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months or use immediately after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with menopause because they offer a natural and gentle way to alleviate symptoms. The herb's calming properties can soothe hot flashes, night sweats, and restlessness, promoting a sense of relaxation and tranquility. Additionally, lemon balm's mild sedative effects can help regulate sleep patterns and reduce anxiety, common complaints during this life stage. By incorporating lemon balm essential oils into daily routines, women can find relief from the discomforts of menopause and enjoy a smoother transition through this significant change.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm essential oil's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can alleviate symptoms such as hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings associated with menopause.

Anxiolytic and calming effects: The anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and calming effects of lemon balm essential oil can help to soothe anxiety, insomnia, and irritability commonly experienced during menopause.

Uterine-relaxing properties: Lemon balm essential oil's uterine-relaxing properties can help to alleviate menstrual cramps, bloating, and other pelvic discomforts associated with menopause by reducing uterine spasms and tension.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, roots help to soothe hot flashes and night sweats associated with menopause.
Flowers: Containing high levels of citral and geraniol, flowers promote relaxation and reduce anxiety symptoms commonly experienced during menopause.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves at dawn when dew is still present for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at low heat for 2 hours.
Steam distill 10 grams of dried melissa officinalis leaves with 100 ml of distilled water for 1 hour.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid, refrigerated at 4°c for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with menopause because of its soothing and calming properties that can ease hot flashes, mood swings, and insomnia common during this stage. The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds also alleviate vaginal dryness and discomfort associated with hormone fluctuations. Additionally, chamomile's sedative effects promote relaxation, reducing anxiety and promoting a restful night's sleep, allowing women to better cope with the physical and emotional changes of menopause.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile oil has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate hot flashes and night sweats commonly experienced during menopause.
Antioxidant property: As an antioxidant, chamomile oil helps protect the body from oxidative stress, which can contribute to menopausal symptoms such as joint pain and fatigue.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) property: Chamomile oil has a calming effect on the mind and body, making it effective in reducing anxiety, mood swings, and irritability often associated with menopause.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin and other bioactive compounds that help alleviate hot flashes, anxiety, and insomnia common during menopause.
Flowers: Contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe vaginal dryness, itching, and burning sensations experienced by many women during menopause.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in late morning for maximum oil yield.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator set at 40°c for 24 hours to preserve their potency.
Combine one part of dried flowers with three parts of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate oil extraction and infusion.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another container and discard the solids; store the resulting essential oil in a dark glass bottle at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle essential oils helps with menopause because it possesses potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe hormonal imbalances, alleviating symptoms such as hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings. The oil's phytoestrogens also help to rebalance estrogen levels, reducing the severity of vaginal dryness and itching. Additionally, milk thistle essential oils may support liver health, which is crucial during menopause when hormonal fluctuations can put extra stress on this vital organ.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Milk thistle essential oil helps neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can alleviate symptoms like hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings associated with menopause.

Hormone regulation: The essential oil's estrogen-regulating properties may help balance hormonal imbalances and alleviate symptoms like vaginal dryness, mood changes, and sleep disturbances during menopause.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Milk thistle essential oil's anti-inflammatory effects can reduce inflammation and discomfort in the body, which may alleviate symptoms like joint pain, cramps, and headaches common during menopause.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silymarin, a powerful antioxidant that helps alleviate hot flashes and night sweats commonly experienced during menopause.
Seeds: Abundant in omega-3 fatty acids, which help reduce inflammation and promote hormonal balance, alleviating symptoms of menopause such as mood swings and joint pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality silybum marianum leaves and flowers at dawn on a dry day.
Dry the plant material in a well-ventilated area or using a dehydrator for 7 days.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a clevenger apparatus at 100°c for 4 hours.
Collect the essential oil and separate it from the water using a separating funnel within 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into dark glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lime essential oils helps with menopause because it has a calming effect on the body, reducing symptoms of hot flashes and night sweats. The citrus scent also boosts mood and energy levels, helping to alleviate feelings of anxiety and depression that are common during this stage of life. Additionally, lime oil's antispasmodic properties help to ease menstrual cramps and promote relaxation, making it a natural remedy for women going through menopause.

Uterine Stimulating Properties: The uterine stimulating properties of herbal lime essential oil can help regulate hormonal imbalances, reducing symptoms such as mood swings, anxiety, and depression that occur during menopause.
Anti-Anxiety and Sedative Properties: Herbal lime essential oil's anti-anxiety and sedative properties can help reduce stress, anxiety, and insomnia, common issues faced by women during menopause.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lime plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, making them effective in reducing hot flashes and night sweats associated with menopause.
Barks: The barks of the lime plant have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate vaginal dryness and itching common during menopause.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tilia x europaea flowers and leaves in the morning to ensure maximum potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using a warm air circulating dryer at 60 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a copper still with a condenser coil and collect the essential oil.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities or debris.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony essential oils helps with menopause because it has been traditionally used in traditional Chinese medicine to regulate women's health, particularly during menopause. Its properties are said to ease symptoms such as hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings by balancing hormones and calming the nervous system. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties may help alleviate vaginal dryness and other physical discomforts associated with menopause. By using this oil, women can find relief from these common symptoms and regain their sense of well-being during this transition period.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: This property helps alleviate hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings associated with menopause by reducing inflammation in the body.

Estrogen-regulating property: Chinese Peony essential oil contains compounds that may help regulate estrogen levels, which can alleviate symptoms such as vaginal dryness, insomnia, and anxiety common during menopause.
Anxiolytic (anxiety-relieving) property: The oil's ability to calm the nervous system can help reduce anxiety, irritability, and mood swings often experienced during menopausal transition.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Chinese Peony contain compounds that help alleviate hot flashes and night sweats, common symptoms of menopause.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe vaginal dryness and irritation associated with menopause.
Seeds: Seeds are rich in estrogenic compounds that help regulate hormonal balance and alleviate symptoms such as mood swings and anxiety during menopause.
remedy_recipe: Harvest paeonia lactiflora flowers in the early morning when dew is still present on the petals ensuring maximum essential oil content.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator at 30°c for 24 hours or air drying for 7 days to preserve quality.
Blend 100g of dried paeonia lactiflora flowers with 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a dark glass bottle.
Infuse the mixture in a cool dark place for 14-16 days shaking the bottle daily allowing the herbs to steep and release their oils.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean dark glass bottle discarding the solids and storing the essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica essential oils helps with menopause because it contains a unique blend of compounds that can alleviate symptoms such as hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings. The oil's natural estrogen-like properties help to regulate hormonal imbalances, reducing the severity of these symptoms. Additionally, angelica's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties can soothe cramps, joint pain, and vaginal dryness, making it a natural solution for women experiencing discomfort during menopause.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) properties: Angelica essential oil has been shown to reduce anxiety and stress, which are common complaints during menopause, by promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system.
Estrogen-like effects: Angelica essential oil contains compounds that have estrogenic activity, which can help alleviate hot flashes and night sweats, as well as improve mood and sleep quality.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Angelica essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce joint pain and discomfort commonly experienced during menopause, as well as alleviate vaginal dryness and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the angelica plant contain bioactive compounds that help alleviate hot flashes and night sweats associated with menopause.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes, which are underground stems, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe hormonal imbalances and reduce symptoms of menopause.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality angelica archangelica roots and leaves from a trusted source for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm and dry place for at least 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces and pack it tightly into a clean glass jar or container leaving little headspace.
Steam distill the packed plant material using a steam distillation apparatus with a ratio of 1:3 water to plant material for 2-3 hours.
Filter the distilled essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth and store it in a dark glass bottle at room temperature away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Monk's pepper essential oils helps with menopause because it is rich in phytochemicals that have a natural affinity for hormone regulation. The oil's bioactive compounds, including cineole and linalool, work to balance the body's hormonal equilibrium, alleviating symptoms such as hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings associated with perimenopause and menopause. Additionally, monk's pepper essential oils has been shown to stimulate blood flow and reduce inflammation, further supporting women's overall health and well-being during this transformative period.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Monk's Pepper essential oil helps combat oxidative stress and inflammation associated with menopause, which can contribute to hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings.

Hormone-regulating properties: The oil's phytoestrogen content helps balance estrogen levels, alleviating symptoms such as hot flashes, vaginal dryness, and mood changes, which are common during menopause.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Monk's Pepper essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce joint pain and stiffness, which many women experience during menopause due to hormonal changes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are used to create a soothing and calming oil that helps alleviate hot flashes and night sweats.
Seeds: Containing potent antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, seeds are used to create an oil that supports hormonal balance and reduces symptoms of menopause.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried cnidium monnieri roots, stems, and leaves for distillation purposes.
Combine the harvested plant material with 1000ml of distilled water in a stainless steel distillation apparatus.
Steam distill the mixture at atmospheric pressure for 2 hours and 30 minutes to extract essential oils.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through activated charcoal and cotton pads to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, away from direct sunlight, at a temperature between 15°c to 25°c for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats essential oils helps with menopause because it has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalance. The oil contains saponins, which help to calm inflammation and reduce hot flashes, a common symptom of menopause. Additionally, oats essential oils have been shown to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality, which can be disrupted during menopause. Its anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe vaginal dryness and itching, providing relief from discomfort associated with this stage of life.

Seeds: Oat seeds contain avenanthramides, compounds that help alleviate symptoms of vaginal dryness and itching during menopause.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh oats avena sativa leaves and flowers in the morning when dew is present.
Dry the plant material using air circulation or shade drying for at least 24 hours.
Steam distill 100g of dried oats avena sativa material using 500ml of water for 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil using paper filter or cotton cloth and discard the sediment.
Store the obtained essential oil in a dark glass bottle with tight lid at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree essential oils helps with menopause because they possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalance. The oil's ability to calm nervous tension and reduce anxiety can bring relief from hot flashes and mood swings, while its estrogen-balancing effects help regulate vaginal dryness and discomfort. Additionally, the oil's antimicrobial properties may help combat yeast infections common during menopause. Overall, maidenhair tree essential oils provide a natural and effective way to manage the physical and emotional challenges of menopause.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: The essential oil of Adiantum giganteum has anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings commonly associated with menopause.

Antioxidant: This plant's essential oil is rich in antioxidants that can protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of menopausal symptoms like joint pain and fatigue.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a reputable source in a clean environment.
Combine 10 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or grapeseed oil.
Allow the mixture to steep for 24 hours at room temperature before shaking and refrigerating overnight.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids after 2 weeks.
Store the herbal ginkgo biloba essential oil in an amber glass bottle in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat menopause with herbal essential oils is by using a blend of Clary Sage, Lavender, and Geranium. These oils work synergistically to alleviate hot flashes, mood swings, and sleep disturbances commonly associated with menopause. Simply apply topically or inhale the aroma to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and balance hormones naturally. Regular use can also help alleviate symptoms like night sweats, vaginal dryness, and joint pain.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to menopause that are treated with herbal essential oils are perimenopause symptoms, menstrual cramps, and premenstrual syndrome (PMS). Clary sage oil, for example, has been shown to ease hot flashes and night sweats, while lavender oil can help alleviate anxiety and insomnia. Peppermint oil is also used to reduce menstrual cramp pain and discomfort, and geranium oil may help regulate hormonal imbalances that contribute to PMS symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cimicifuga racemosa essential oils helps with menopause because it contains compounds that mimic estrogen, alleviating symptoms of hormonal imbalance. It reduces hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings by promoting a sense of calm and relaxation. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties also help to ease joint pain and stiffness, common complaints during menopause. Additionally, Cimicifuga racemosa essential oils have been shown to support the body's natural hormonal balance, providing relief from menopausal symptoms and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Isoferulic acid: This phenolic compound is believed to help alleviate menopausal symptoms by reducing inflammation and modulating hormonal balance.
Rotundifuran: This phenolic compound is thought to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which may help alleviate hot flashes and other symptoms associated with menopause.

Actein: This triterpenoid saponin is believed to have a direct impact on the body's hormonal balance, particularly estrogen levels, which may help alleviate menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes and night sweats.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in actein, a compound believed to help alleviate menopausal symptoms.
Leaves: They contain various bioactive compounds, including ferulic acid, which may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help with menopause symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 50g of dried cimicifuga racemosa root material in the fall season.
Steam distill the root material at a temperature of 100-110°c for 2-3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from water using a separator.
Filter the essential oil through a 10μm filter paper to remove any impurities and particles.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle at a temperature below 30°c for future use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus essential oils helps with menopause because it is believed to support hormonal balance and alleviate symptoms associated with this life stage. The oil is thought to influence the regulation of estrogen levels, reducing hot flashes and night sweats. Additionally, it may help stabilize mood and reduce anxiety, promoting a sense of calm and well-being. Some studies suggest that Vitex agnus-castus may also help to alleviate menstrual cramps and breast tenderness, making it a popular natural remedy for menopausal women.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help with menopause by regulating hormonal imbalances, particularly estrogen levels, which can alleviate symptoms such as hot flashes and mood swings.
Phenolic acids: They assist in reducing inflammation and modulating the body's response to hormonal fluctuations, contributing to relief from menopausal symptoms like anxiety and sleep disturbances.

Diterpenoids: These constituents have a balancing effect on the body's hormonal system, particularly targeting the regulation of luteinizing hormone (LH) levels, which can help alleviate symptoms of menopause such as irregular menstrual cycles and hot flashes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are primarily used for their therapeutic benefits due to their high concentration of iridoid glycosides, which are believed to have a positive effect on menopausal symptoms.

Leaves: The leaves are used to create essential oils that contain a combination of iridoid glycosides and flavonoids, which are thought to help alleviate menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes and mood swings.

Seeds: The seeds are used to produce essential oils that are rich in iridoid glycosides, which are believed to have a regulating effect on hormonal balance, thus helping to alleviate menopausal symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 kg of dried vitex agnus-castus flowers at the peak of blooming season.
Steam distill the dried flowers at 100°c for 3 hours to extract the essential oil.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the hydrosol using a separator.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter to remove impurities and debris.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils helps with menopause because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can alleviate symptoms such as hot flashes and night sweats. The licorice root's natural compounds, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, may also help regulate hormonal imbalances and reduce stress levels. Additionally, this essential oil may improve sleep quality and reduce anxiety, which can be particularly beneficial during menopause. Its calming and soothing effects can promote a sense of well-being and overall health.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizic Acid: This constituent helps with menopause by reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of hot flashes and night sweats due to its anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidant properties.
Licopyranocoumarin: This constituent helps with menopause by exerting a sedative effect, which can aid in reducing anxiety and insomnia associated with menopause.
Isoliquiritigenin: This constituent helps with menopause by exhibiting estrogenic activity, which can help alleviate menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are commonly used to make essential oils due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, which helps to alleviate menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes and mood swings.

Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used for essential oils due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and balance hormonal fluctuations during menopause.
Flowers: The flowers of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used for essential oils due to their calming and mood-balancing effects, which help to alleviate anxiety and depression often associated with menopause.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of clean dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra at dawn when the dew is still present.
Steam distill the roots in a copper still at a temperature between 100c to 110c for 4 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separating funnel.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid at a temperature not exceeding 30c.
Allow the essential oil to mature for a minimum of 30 days before using it for aromatherapy purposes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica archangelica essential oils helps with menopause because it contains compounds that mimic estrogen, providing relief from hot flashes and night sweats. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe hormonal imbalances, reducing symptoms of anxiety and mood swings. Additionally, its antispasmodic properties ease menstrual cramps and promote relaxation, making it a natural remedy for women experiencing menopausal symptoms. remedy_constituents: Phenolic glycosides: These compounds help alleviate menopause symptoms by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce hot flashes and night sweats.
Coumarins: Coumarins in Angelica archangelica essential oils have been shown to have a sedative effect, which can help reduce anxiety and insomnia often associated with menopause.
Phytosterols: Phytosterols in the essential oil have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate hot flashes, mood swings, and other menopause symptoms.

Stems: The stems are used for their high concentration of terpenes and sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help reduce menopause-related anxiety and mood swings.

remedy_recipe: Harvest mature angelica archangelica roots and leaves in late summer when the plant is in full bloom for 2 weeks.
Dry the collected plant material in a warm dry place for 1 to 2 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried angelica archangelica roots and leaves at 100 to 110 degrees celsius for 2 to 3 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and separate it from the water and other plant materials using a separator device.
Filter the essential oil through a paper or membrane filter to remove any remaining impurities and debris.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oils helps with menopause because it contains valerenic acid, which has a sedative and calming effect on the body. This can help alleviate symptoms such as hot flashes and night sweats, allowing women to get a more restful sleep. Additionally, valerian root has been shown to regulate hormonal imbalances associated with menopause, promoting a sense of balance and calmness. remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Helps with menopause by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation due to its sedative properties.
Valeranone: Aids in menopause symptoms by acting as a GABA receptor agonist, which helps to reduce hot flashes and night sweats.
Valerenol: Contributes to menopause relief by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms such as joint pain and mood swings.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Valeriana officinalis are commonly used due to their high concentration of valerenic acid, which is known for its sedative and calming properties.
Roots: The roots are also rich in valeranone, which has been shown to have anti-anxiety and sleep-promoting effects, making them beneficial for menopause symptoms.
Roots: The root's valerenic acid and valeranone content also contributes to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate menopause-related pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots and rhizomes in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal oil yield.
Steam distill the harvested plant material in a 0.5:1 ratio of plant to water for 2 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water and plant residue.
Filter the essential oil through a 50-micron filter to remove impurities and ensure clarity.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with menopause because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that alleviate hot flashes and night sweats. remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Help alleviate menopausal symptoms by reducing inflammation and pain associated with hot flashes and menstrual cramps.
Shogaols: Act as antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, which may help reduce the severity and frequency of hot flashes and night sweats during menopause.
Bisabolol: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, potentially aiding in the relief of menopausal symptoms such as mood swings and vaginal dryness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are also used to create essential oils that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate menstrual cramps, mood swings, and other menopause-related issues.
Flowers: Zingiber officinale flowers are used to make essential oils that have a calming effect, helping to reduce anxiety, stress, and hot flashes associated with menopause.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the zingiber officinale roots in the early morning and clean them thoroughly to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the zingiber officinale roots in a well-ventilated area for 2-3 weeks to reduce moisture content to 15%.
Steam distill the dried zingiber officinale roots at 100-150 degrees celsius to extract the essential oil.
Collect the zingiber officinale essential oil through condensation and filter it using a coffee filter paper to remove impurities.
Store the extracted zingiber officinale essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Trifolium pratense essential oils helps with menopause because it is rich in estrogen-balancing properties, which can alleviate hot flashes and night sweats. The oil's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties also help to soothe and calm the body, reducing anxiety and stress that often accompany menopause. Additionally, its ability to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality can help alleviate symptoms such as mood swings and fatigue, making it a natural and effective solution for managing menopausal symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavones: These plant-derived compounds help alleviate menopausal symptoms by acting as estrogen agonists, which can alleviate hot flashes, night sweats, and mood changes associated with estrogen decline.
Furostanol saponins: These saponins help to reduce hot flashes and night sweats by modulating the body's response to estrogen fluctuations and improving sleep quality.

Genistein: This isoflavone acts as a phytoestrogen, helping to mitigate the effects of estrogen deficiency on menopausal symptoms, particularly hot flashes and mood swings, by binding to estrogen receptors and modulating hormone production.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make essential oils that may help alleviate menopausal symptoms due to their estrogenic properties.
Leaves: Leaves of Trifolium pratense are used to create essential oils that may provide relief from menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes and night sweats.
Roots: Roots of the plant are used to make essential oils that may help balance hormone levels and reduce menopausal symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh trifolium pratense flowers from a pesticide-free area at dawn when dew is still present.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried flowers in a 1:3 ratio of plant material to distilled water for 2 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water by decanting the oil into a separate container.
Store the extracted trifolium pratense essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from direct sunlight and heat sources.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa essential oils helps with menopause because it contains a unique blend of phytoestrogens and saponins, which work to regulate hormonal imbalances and alleviate symptoms associated with menopause. The oil's calming and soothing properties can help reduce anxiety and stress, while its anti-inflammatory properties may ease hot flashes and night sweats. Additionally, Avena sativa essential oils may also help to improve sleep quality and promote relaxation, making it a natural and holistic approach to managing menopause symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Ferulic acid: Helps with menopause by reducing hot flashes and night sweats due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Lignans: Assists in alleviating menopausal symptoms, such as hot flashes and mood swings, by acting as a natural estrogen and modulating hormonal balance.
Beta-sitosterol: Contributes to menopausal relief by reducing inflammation and improving sleep quality, thereby alleviating hot flashes and night sweats.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Avena sativa flowers are commonly used to make essential oils for menopause due to their high content of isoflavones, which may help alleviate menopausal symptoms.
Leaves: Avena sativa leaves are often used to create essential oils for menopause because they contain sesquiterpenes, which may help reduce hot flashes and other symptoms.

Seeds: Avena sativa seeds are used to make essential oils for menopause due to their high content of fatty acids and phytosterols, which may help alleviate menopausal symptoms and improve hormone balance.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature avena sativa stems and flowers at dawn to preserve their potency and fragrance.
Dry the harvested material in a warm place for 1-2 weeks or until it reaches a moisture level of 10 percent.
Steam distill the dried avena sativa material using a still with a 10 kg distillation capacity for 3 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil in a glass container and filter it using a 10 micron filter paper within 2 hours.
Store the final avena sativa essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at a temperature below 25 degrees celsius.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum essential oils helps with menopause because it has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalances. The oil contains flavonoids and hyperforin, which have been found to have estrogen-balancing properties, helping to reduce hot flashes and night sweats. Additionally, its adaptogenic properties allow the body to cope with stress, promoting a sense of calm and well-being. This natural remedy can also help to regulate mood swings and insomnia, providing relief during this transitional period in a woman's life.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Acts as a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to alleviate hot flashes and night sweats associated with menopause.
N-feruloylserotonin: Exhibits serotonin-releasing properties, which may help to improve mood and reduce symptoms of depression often experienced during menopause.
Quercetin: Demonstrates estrogen-regulating and anti-inflammatory properties, potentially helping to alleviate menopausal symptoms such as mood swings and vaginal dryness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to create essential oils that help with hot flashes and night sweats associated with menopause due to their high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Flowers: The flowers of Hypericum perforatum are used to make essential oils that alleviate menopause symptoms such as anxiety and mood swings due to their high concentration of hypericin and hyperforin.
Roots: The roots are used to create essential oils that help with hormonal balance during menopause due to their high content of sesquiterpenes and phenolic compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Steam distill the fresh flowers in a 30:1 ratio with distilled water at 100°c for 2 hours.
Collect the distilled water and separate the essential oil from the hydrosol using a separator.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle at 4°c in a cool dry place.
Label the essential oil bottle with the name hypericum perforatum essential oil and the date of distillation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils helps with menopause because of its adaptogenic and calming properties, which can alleviate symptoms such as hot flashes and mood swings. The oil's high concentration of linalool and linalyl acetate helps to balance hormones and reduce inflammation, promoting relaxation and emotional well-being. Additionally, its sweet, lemony scent can help to uplift and calm the mind and body, providing a sense of relief and tranquility during this life transition. This natural remedy can be a valuable addition to menopause symptom management.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps reduce hot flashes and night sweats associated with menopause due to its antispasmodic and anxiolytic properties.
Rosenol: A phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of menopause such as mood swings and hormonal imbalances.
Geraniol: Exhibits estrogenic activity, which can help balance hormonal levels and alleviate symptoms of menopause such as hot flashes and vaginal dryness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are primarily used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds such as linalool and rosmarinic acid, which are known for their therapeutic benefits.
Flowers: The flowers are utilized for their calming and anti-anxiety properties, which can help alleviate hot flashes and mood swings associated with menopause.
Stems: The stems contain a significant amount of essential oils, including linalool and beta-pinene, which contribute to their therapeutic effects in menopause relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature melissa officinalis flowers when they are in full bloom and free of any damage.
Steam distill the harvested flowers using a distillation apparatus with a 3:1 flower-to-water ratio.
Collect the distilled steam and condense it into a separate container for oil separation.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separating funnel over a 30-minute period.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid immediately.
